{{出典の明記|date=2016年10月}} {{公園 |名称 = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |英語名称 = Tamateyama Park |画像 = [[ファイル:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |画像キャプション = 中央ゲート |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[大阪府]][[柏原市]]玉手町7-1 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 42.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 42.0 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 約68,000m<sup>2</sup> |前身 = [[近鉄玉手山遊園地]] |開園 = [[1999年]][[3月]] |運営者 = 柏原市役所公園緑地課 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |駐車場 = あり(30台) |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 玉手山公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 大阪府柏原市玉手町7-1 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス|参照]] |備考 = 毎週水曜休園 |公式サイト = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm 柏原市:市立玉手山公園] }} '''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（かしわらしりつ たまてやまこうえん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある玉手山丘陵地帯にある[[都市公園]]（総合公園）<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html 大阪府地図情報システム（都市計画情報）（大阪府）]</ref>で、1999年（平成11年）3月に開園した。
==沿革・概要== 前身は1908年（明治41年）に[[大阪鉄道 (2代目)|河南鉄道]]（現在の[[近鉄南大阪線]]）によって開設された西日本で最も古い[[遊園地]]である[[近鉄玉手山遊園地]]。近鉄玉手山遊園地は1998年（平成10年）5月31日に90年の歴史を閉じた。なお、敷地の大部分を所有する[[宗教法人]]である[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の檀家からの要請で、柏原市が近鉄から遊戯具等の設備を含むほぼそのままの形で運営を引き継ぐこととなり、約1年間の休園の後、1999年（平成11年）3月に'''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（「ふれあいぱーく」）として開業した。
なお、68,000平方メートルの敷地の大部分は地元の[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の所有地であり、かつては近鉄興業（当時）が安福寺から借地しており、現在は柏原市が安福寺から、年間670万円程の賃貸料で借地している。
==立地環境== [[画像:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb||right|アンティックな音楽堂]] *公園内、東入口付近を中心に「[[玉手山古墳群]]」とよばれる[[古墳]]群が存在する。 *公園内の高台には「アンティックな音楽堂」とよばれる、1908年の開園当時の[[ガゼボ]]がある。明治の洋風建築様式の面影が色濃く残っている。また、天辺に河南鉄道の社印が取り付けられ、現在も残っている。 *かつて玉手山付近と石川の対岸にある[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]付近にかけては[[大坂夏の陣]]において[[道明寺の戦い]]が行われた。豊臣方の[[後藤基次|後藤基次（又兵衛）]]は玉手山（旧称：小松山）に陣を敷き、徳川方と戦って戦死した。公園内に両軍戦死者の供養塔、後藤又兵衛の碑や後藤又兵衛しだれ桜がある。 *俳人[[小林一茶]]のゆかりの地でもあり、寛政7年（1795年）4月3日に[[葛井寺]]と道明寺に詣でた後、当時すでに景勝地として有名になっていた玉手山を訪れている。ここで俳句を二句詠んでおり、そのうちの一つを刻んだ句碑が公園内にある。 *敷地内には、[[広域放送|在阪]][[テレビ局]]の[[柏原テレビ中継局]]がある。
==問題点・課題== {{出典の明記|date=2016年10月|section=1}} *掛かりすぎる公園維持管理費 :平成19年度予算で見ると玉手山公園関連維持管理費は3,138万円、これは柏原市の公園維持管理費総額7,627万円の実に41%を占めている。その内柏原市所有の敷地・建物に設置されている有料遊戯具の維持管理も含め業者への管理委託費は2,120万円に上り玉手山公園維持管理費の実に67.4%を占め、柏原市の所有する全公園の維持管理委託費の実に、36.4%（平成19年度）にも登っている。。 *市立の一般公園であるにもかかわらず警備上の理由から、夜間は閉門し立ち入り禁止であるため、帰宅後や早朝出勤前のジョギング、ウォーキング等の公園本来の目的に使用できない。 *公共施設であるにも関わらず、野外劇場などの利用規程がはっきりしていない。 *有料遊戯具の設置に関して委託業者との契約内容が公開されておらず、料金収入の行方等、不明朗な点が多々見られる。 *来園者の殆どが近隣市町村からの外来者で占められ、柏原市民の利用率が極端に低いこと。 *たのしみ館、昆虫館等の常設館は果たして必要なのか。特に昆虫館については、展示物の管轄を教育委員会にゆだね、他の施設に移転すべきではないか。 *老巧化の激しい、野外劇場その他の建造物をどうするか。建物を解体整理し維持管理費の縮小を諮るべきでは無いか。 *国的に見ても、前述した宗教法人からの借地契約は地方自治体の運営する公立公園としては異例である。
等問題が山積している。また、
*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Tamateyama koen}} *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ 市立玉手山公園] *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ 玉手山公園と玉手山古墳群、玉手山(安福寺)横穴群]
{{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまてやまこうえん}} [[category:大阪府の公園]] [[category:柏原市の地理]]

*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Tamateyama koen}} *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ 市立玉手山公園] *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ 玉手山公園と玉手山古墳群、玉手山(安福寺)横穴群]
{{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまてやまこうえん}} [[category:大阪府の公園]] [[category:柏原市の地理]]

{{出典の明記|date=2016年10月}} {{公園 |名称 = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |英語名称 = Tamateyama Park |画像 = [[ファイル:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |画像キャプション = 中央ゲート |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[大阪府]][[柏原市]]玉手町7-1 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 42.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 42.0 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 約68,000m<sup>2</sup> |前身 = [[近鉄玉手山遊園地]] |開園 = [[1999年]][[3月]] |運営者 = 柏原市役所公園緑地課 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |駐車場 = あり(30台) |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 玉手山公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 大阪府柏原市玉手町7-1 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス|参照]] |備考 = 毎週水曜休園 |公式サイト = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm 柏原市:市立玉手山公園] }} '''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（かしわらしりつ たまてやまこうえん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある玉手山丘陵地帯にある[[都市公園]]（総合公園）<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html 大阪府地図情報システム（都市計画情報）（大阪府）]</ref>で、1999年（平成11年）3月に開園した。
==沿革・概要== 前身は1908年（明治41年）に[[大阪鉄道 (2代目)|河南鉄道]]（現在の[[近鉄南大阪線]]）によって開設された西日本で最も古い[[遊園地]]である[[近鉄玉手山遊園地]]。近鉄玉手山遊園地は1998年（平成10年）5月31日に90年の歴史を閉じた。なお、敷地の大部分を所有する[[宗教法人]]である[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の檀家からの要請で、柏原市が近鉄から遊戯具等の設備を含むほぼそのままの形で運営を引き継ぐこととなり、約1年間の休園の後、1999年（平成11年）3月に'''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（「ふれあいぱーく」）として開業した。
なお、68,000平方メートルの敷地の大部分は地元の[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の所有地であり、かつては近鉄興業（当時）が安福寺から借地しており、現在は柏原市が安福寺から、年間670万円程の賃貸料で借地している。
==立地環境== [[画像:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|アンティックな音楽堂]] *公園内、東入口付近を中心に「[[玉手山古墳群]]」とよばれる[[古墳]]群が存在する。 *公園内の高台には「アンティックな音楽堂」とよばれる、1908年の開園当時の[[ガゼボ]]がある。明治の洋風建築様式の面影が色濃く残っている。また、天辺に河南鉄道の社印が取り付けられ、現在も残っている。 *かつて玉手山付近と石川の対岸にある[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]付近にかけては[[大坂夏の陣]]において[[道明寺の戦い]]が行われた。豊臣方の[[後藤基次|後藤基次（又兵衛）]]は玉手山（旧称：小松山）に陣を敷き、徳川方と戦って戦死した。公園内に両軍戦死者の供養塔、後藤又兵衛の碑や後藤又兵衛しだれ桜がある。 *俳人[[小林一茶]]のゆかりの地でもあり、寛政7年（1795年）4月3日に[[葛井寺]]と道明寺に詣でた後、当時すでに景勝地として有名になっていた玉手山を訪れている。ここで俳句を二句詠んでおり、そのうちの一つを刻んだ句碑が公園内にある。 *敷地内には、[[広域放送|在阪]][[テレビ局]]の[[柏原テレビ中継局]]がある。
==問題点・課題== {{出典の明記|date=2016年10月|section=1}} *掛かりすぎる公園維持管理費 :平成19年度予算で見ると玉手山公園関連維持管理費は3,138万円、これは柏原市の公園維持管理費総額7,627万円の実に41%を占めている。その内柏原市所有の敷地・建物に設置されている有料遊戯具の維持管理も含め業者への管理委託費は2,120万円に上り玉手山公園維持管理費の実に67.4%を占め、柏原市の所有する全公園の維持管理委託費の実に、36.4%（平成19年度）にも登っている。。 *市立の一般公園であるにもかかわらず警備上の理由から、夜間は閉門し立ち入り禁止であるため、帰宅後や早朝出勤前のジョギング、ウォーキング等の公園本来の目的に使用できない。 *公共施設であるにも関わらず、野外劇場などの利用規程がはっきりしていない。 *有料遊戯具の設置に関して委託業者との契約内容が公開されていない。 *来園者の殆どが近隣市町村からの外来者で占められ、柏原市民の利用率が極端に低いこと。 *たのしみ館、昆虫館等の常設館の設置意義や老巧化の激しい野外劇場その他の建造物。
等の問題がある。また、
*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Tamateyama koen}} *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ 市立玉手山公園] *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ 玉手山公園と玉手山古墳群、玉手山(安福寺)横穴群]
{{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまてやまこうえん}} [[category:大阪府の公園]] [[category:柏原市の地理]] [[Category:1999年開業の施設]]

{{出典の明記|date=2016年10月}} {{公園 |名称 = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |英語名称 = Tamateyama Park |画像 = [[ファイル:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |画像キャプション = 中央ゲート |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[大阪府]][[柏原市]]玉手町7-1 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 42.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 42.0 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 約68,000m<sup>2</sup> |前身 = [[近鉄玉手山遊園地]] |開園 = [[1999年]][[3月]] |運営者 = 柏原市役所公園緑地課 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |駐車場 = あり(30台) |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 玉手山公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 大阪府柏原市玉手町7-1 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス|参照]] |備考 = 毎週水曜休園 |公式サイト = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm 柏原市:市立玉手山公園] }} '''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（かしわらしりつ たまてやまこうえん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある玉手山丘陵地帯にある[[都市公園]]（総合公園）<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html 大阪府地図情報システム（都市計画情報）（大阪府）]</ref>で、1999年（平成11年）3月に開園した。
==沿革・概要== 前身は1908年（明治41年）に[[大阪鉄道 (2代目)|河南鉄道]]（現在の[[近鉄南大阪線]]）によって開設された西日本で最も古い[[遊園地]]である[[近鉄玉手山遊園地]]。近鉄玉手山遊園地は1998年（平成10年）5月31日に90年の歴史を閉じた。なお、敷地の大部分を所有する[[宗教法人]]である[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の檀家からの要請で、柏原市が近鉄から遊戯具等の設備を含むほぼそのままの形で運営を引き継ぐこととなり、約1年間の休園の後、1999年（平成11年）3月に'''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（「ふれあいぱーく」）として開業した。
なお、68,000平方メートルの敷地の大部分は地元の[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の所有地であり、かつては近鉄興業（当時）が安福寺から借地しており、現在は柏原市が安福寺から、年間670万円程の賃貸料で借地している。
==立地環境== [[画像:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|アンティックな音楽堂]] *公園内、東入口付近を中心に「[[玉手山古墳群]]」とよばれる[[古墳]]群が存在する。 *公園内の高台には「アンティックな音楽堂」とよばれる、1908年の開園当時の[[ガゼボ]]がある。明治の洋風建築様式の面影が色濃く残っている。また、天辺に河南鉄道の社印が取り付けられ、現在も残っている。 *かつて玉手山付近と石川の対岸にある[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]付近にかけては[[大坂の陣#大坂夏の陣|大坂夏の陣]]において[[道明寺の戦い]]が行われた。豊臣方の[[後藤基次|後藤基次（又兵衛）]]は玉手山（旧称：小松山）に陣を敷き、徳川方と戦って戦死した。公園内に両軍戦死者の供養塔、後藤又兵衛の碑や後藤又兵衛しだれ桜がある。 *俳人[[小林一茶]]のゆかりの地でもあり、寛政7年（1795年）4月3日に[[葛井寺]]と道明寺に詣でた後、当時すでに景勝地として有名になっていた玉手山を訪れている。ここで俳句を二句詠んでおり、そのうちの一つを刻んだ句碑が公園内にある。 *敷地内には、[[広域放送|在阪]][[テレビ局]]の[[柏原テレビ中継局]]がある。
==問題点・課題== {{出典の明記|date=2016年10月|section=1}} *掛かりすぎる公園維持管理費 :平成19年度予算で見ると玉手山公園関連維持管理費は3,138万円、これは柏原市の公園維持管理費総額7,627万円の実に41%を占めている。その内柏原市所有の敷地・建物に設置されている有料遊戯具の維持管理も含め業者への管理委託費は2,120万円に上り玉手山公園維持管理費の実に67.4%を占め、柏原市の所有する全公園の維持管理委託費の実に、36.4%（平成19年度）にも登っている。。 *市立の一般公園であるにもかかわらず警備上の理由から、夜間は閉門し立ち入り禁止であるため、帰宅後や早朝出勤前のジョギング、ウォーキング等の公園本来の目的に使用できない。 *公共施設であるにも関わらず、野外劇場などの利用規程がはっきりしていない。 *有料遊戯具の設置に関して委託業者との契約内容が公開されていない。 *来園者の殆どが近隣市町村からの外来者で占められ、柏原市民の利用率が極端に低いこと。 *たのしみ館、昆虫館等の常設館の設置意義や老朽化の激しい野外劇場その他の建造物。
等の問題がある。また、
*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Tamateyama koen}} *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ 市立玉手山公園] *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ 玉手山公園と玉手山古墳群、玉手山(安福寺)横穴群]

==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Tamateyama koen}} *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ 市立玉手山公園] *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ 玉手山公園と玉手山古墳群、玉手山(安福寺)横穴群]
{{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまてやまこうえん}} [[category:大阪府の公園]] [[category:柏原市の地理]] [[Category:1999年開業の施設]]

{{出典の明記|date=2016年10月}} {{公園 |名称 = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |英語名称 = Tamateyama Park |画像 = [[ファイル:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |画像キャプション = 中央ゲート |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[大阪府]][[柏原市]]玉手町7-1 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 42.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 42.0 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 約68,000m<sup>2</sup> |前身 = [[近鉄玉手山遊園地]] |開園 = [[1999年]][[3月]] |運営者 = 柏原市役所公園緑地課 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 玉手山公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 大阪府柏原市玉手町7-1 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス|参照]] |備考 = 毎週水曜休園 |公式サイト = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm 柏原市:市立玉手山公園] }} '''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（かしわらしりつ たまてやまこうえん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある玉手山丘陵地帯にある[[都市公園]]（総合公園）<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html 大阪府地図情報システム（都市計画情報）（大阪府）]</ref>で、1999年（平成11年）3月に開園した。
==沿革・概要== 前身は1908年（明治41年）に[[大阪鉄道 (2代目)|河南鉄道]]（現在の[[近鉄南大阪線]]）によって開設された西日本で最も古い[[遊園地]]である[[近鉄玉手山遊園地]]。近鉄玉手山遊園地は1998年（平成10年）5月31日に90年の歴史を閉じた。なお、敷地の大部分を所有する[[宗教法人]]である[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の檀家からの要請で、柏原市が近鉄から遊戯具等の設備を含むほぼそのままの形で運営を引き継ぐこととなり、約1年間の休園の後、1999年（平成11年）3月に'''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（「ふれあいぱーく」）として開業した。
なお、68,000平方メートルの敷地の大部分は地元の[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の所有地であり、かつては近鉄興業（当時）が安福寺から借地しており、現在は柏原市が安福寺から、年間670万円程の賃貸料で借地している。
==立地環境== [[画像:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|アンティックな音楽堂]] *公園内、東入口付近を中心に「[[玉手山古墳群]]」とよばれる[[古墳]]群が存在する。 *公園内の高台には「アンティックな音楽堂」とよばれる、1908年の開園当時の[[ガゼボ]]がある。明治の洋風建築様式の面影が色濃く残っている。また、天辺に河南鉄道の社印が取り付けられ、現在も残っている。 *かつて玉手山付近と石川の対岸にある[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]付近にかけては[[大坂の陣#大坂夏の陣|大坂夏の陣]]において[[道明寺の戦い]]が行われた。豊臣方の[[後藤基次|後藤基次（又兵衛）]]は玉手山（旧称：小松山）に陣を敷き、徳川方と戦って戦死した。公園内に両軍戦死者の供養塔、後藤又兵衛の碑や後藤又兵衛しだれ桜がある。 *俳人[[小林一茶]]のゆかりの地でもあり、寛政7年（1795年）4月3日に[[葛井寺]]と道明寺に詣でた後、当時すでに景勝地として有名になっていた玉手山を訪れている。ここで俳句を二句詠んでおり、そのうちの一つを刻んだ句碑が公園内にある。 *敷地内には、[[広域放送|在阪]][[テレビ局]]の[[柏原テレビ中継局]]がある。
==問題点・課題== {{出典の明記|date=2016年10月|section=1}} *掛かりすぎる公園維持管理費 :平成19年度予算で見ると玉手山公園関連維持管理費は3,138万円、これは柏原市の公園維持管理費総額7,627万円の実に41%を占めている。その内柏原市所有の敷地・建物に設置されている有料遊戯具の維持管理も含め業者への管理委託費は2,120万円に上り玉手山公園維持管理費の実に67.4%を占め、柏原市の所有する全公園の維持管理委託費の実に、36.4%（平成19年度）にも登っている。 *市立の一般公園であるにもかかわらず警備上の理由から、夜間は閉門し立ち入り禁止であるため、帰宅後や早朝出勤前のジョギング、ウォーキング等の公園本来の目的に使用できない。 *公共施設であるにもかかわらず、野外劇場などの利用規程がはっきりしていない。 *有料遊戯具の設置に関して委託業者との契約内容が公開されていない。 *来園者の殆どが近隣市町村からの外来者で占められ、柏原市民の利用率が極端に低いこと。 *たのしみ館、昆虫館等の常設館の設置意義や老朽化の激しい野外劇場その他の建造物。
等の問題がある。また、
*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately 68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately 68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

{{Cite sources|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately 68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.

It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

{{出典の明記|date=2016年10月}} {{公園 |名称 = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |英語名称 = Tamateyama Park |画像 = [[ファイル:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |画像キャプション = 中央ゲート |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[大阪府]][[柏原市]]玉手町7-1 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 42.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 42.0 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 約68,000m<sup>2</sup> |前身 = [[近鉄玉手山遊園地]] |開園 = [[1999年]][[3月]] |運営者 = 柏原市役所公園緑地課 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 玉手山公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 大阪府柏原市玉手町7-1 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス|参照]] |備考 = 毎週水曜休園 |公式サイト = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm 柏原市:市立玉手山公園] }} '''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（かしわらしりつ たまてやまこうえん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある玉手山丘陵地帯にある[[都市公園]]（総合公園）<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html 大阪府地図情報システム（都市計画情報）（大阪府）]</ref>で、1999年（平成11年）3月に開園した。
==沿革・概要== 前身は1908年（明治41年）に[[大阪鉄道 (2代目)|河南鉄道]]（現在の[[近鉄南大阪線]]）によって開設された西日本で最も古い[[遊園地]]である[[近鉄玉手山遊園地]]。近鉄玉手山遊園地は1998年（平成10年）5月31日に90年の歴史を閉じた。なお、敷地の大部分を所有する[[宗教法人]]である[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の檀家からの要請で、柏原市が近鉄から遊戯具等の設備を含むほぼそのままの形で運営を引き継ぐこととなり、約1年間の休園の後、1999年（平成11年）3月に'''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（「ふれあいぱーく」）として開業した。
なお、68,000平方メートルの敷地の大部分は地元の[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の所有地であり、かつては近鉄興業（当時）が安福寺から借地しており、現在は柏原市が安福寺から、年間670万円程の賃貸料で借地している。
==立地環境== [[画像:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|アンティックな音楽堂]] *公園内、東入口付近を中心に「[[玉手山古墳群]]」とよばれる[[古墳]]群が存在する。 *公園内の高台には「アンティックな音楽堂」とよばれる、1908年の開園当時の[[ガゼボ]]がある。明治の洋風建築様式の面影が色濃く残っている。また、天辺に河南鉄道の社印が取り付けられ、現在も残っている。 *かつて玉手山付近と石川の対岸にある[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]付近にかけては[[大坂の陣#大坂夏の陣|大坂夏の陣]]において[[道明寺の戦い]]が行われた。豊臣方の[[後藤基次|後藤基次（又兵衛）]]は玉手山（旧称：小松山）に陣を敷き、徳川方と戦って戦死した。公園内に両軍戦死者の供養塔、後藤又兵衛の碑や後藤又兵衛しだれ桜がある。 *俳人[[小林一茶]]のゆかりの地でもあり、寛政7年（1795年）4月3日に[[葛井寺]]と道明寺に詣でた後、当時すでに景勝地として有名になっていた玉手山を訪れている。ここで俳句を二句詠んでおり、そのうちの一つを刻んだ句碑が公園内にある。 *敷地内には、[[広域放送|在阪]][[テレビ局]]の[[柏原テレビ中継局]]がある。
==問題点・課題== {{出典の明記|date=2016年10月|section=1}} *掛かりすぎる公園維持管理費 :平成19年度予算で見ると玉手山公園関連維持管理費は3,138万円、これは柏原市の公園維持管理費総額7,627万円の実に41%を占めている。その内柏原市所有の敷地・建物に設置されている有料遊戯具の維持管理も含め業者への管理委託費は2,120万円に上り玉手山公園維持管理費の実に67.4%を占め、柏原市の所有する全公園の維持管理委託費の実に、36.4%（平成19年度）にも上っている。 *市立の一般公園であるにもかかわらず警備上の理由から、夜間は閉門し立ち入り禁止であるため、帰宅後や早朝出勤前のジョギング、ウォーキング等の公園本来の目的に使用できない。 *公共施設であるにもかかわらず、野外劇場などの利用規程がはっきりしていない。 *有料遊戯具の設置に関して委託業者との契約内容が公開されていない。 *来園者の殆どが近隣市町村からの外来者で占められ、柏原市民の利用率が極端に低いこと。 *たのしみ館、昆虫館等の常設館の設置意義や老朽化の激しい野外劇場その他の建造物。
等の問題がある。また、
*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分

'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient burial mounds known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.

It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. {{Cite sources|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Aqqroximately 68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for aqqroximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})
== References == {{Reflist}}

Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

{{Cite sources|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = A11roximately 68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})

{{Cite sources|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = A11roximately 68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}})
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:11]][[ja:[[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Battle of Domyoji|ja|道明寺の戦い|12=draft}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Goto Matabei|ja|後藤基次|12=draft}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|ja|小林一茶|12=draft}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=draft}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Cite sources|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Domyoji Station|ja|道明寺駅|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|ja|河内国分駅|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft}})

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2016年10月}} {{公園 |名称 = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |英語名称 = Tamateyama Park |画像 = [[ファイル:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |画像キャプション = 中央ゲート |国 = {{JPN}} |都市 = [[大阪府]][[柏原市]]玉手町7-1 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 42.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 42.0 |分類 = [[都市公園]]（総合公園） |面積 = 約68,000m<sup>2</sup> |前身 = [[近鉄玉手山遊園地]] |開園 = [[1999年]][[3月]] |運営者 = 柏原市役所公園緑地課 |年来園者数 = |現況 = |設備・遊具 = |バリアフリー = |建築家と技術者 = |告示 = |事務所 = 玉手山公園事務所 |事務所所在地 = 大阪府柏原市玉手町7-1 |アクセス = [[#交通アクセス|参照]] |備考 = 毎週水曜休園 |公式サイト = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm 柏原市:市立玉手山公園] }} '''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（かしわらしりつ たまてやまこうえん）は、[[大阪府]][[柏原市]]にある玉手山丘陵地帯にある[[都市公園]]（総合公園）<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html 大阪府地図情報システム（都市計画情報）（大阪府）]</ref>で、1999年（平成11年）3月に開園した。
==沿革・概要== 前身は1908年（明治41年）に[[大阪鉄道 (2代目)|河南鉄道]]（現在の[[近鉄南大阪線]]）によって開設された西日本で最も古い[[遊園地]]である[[近鉄玉手山遊園地]]。近鉄玉手山遊園地は1998年（平成10年）5月31日に90年の歴史を閉じた。なお、敷地の大部分を所有する[[宗教法人]]である[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の檀家からの要請で、柏原市が近鉄から遊戯具等の設備を含むほぼそのままの形で運営を引き継ぐこととなり、約1年間の休園の後、1999年（平成11年）3月に'''柏原市立玉手山公園'''（「ふれあいぱーく」）として開業した。
なお、68,000平方メートルの敷地の大部分は地元の[[安福寺 (柏原市)|安福寺]]の所有地であり、かつては近鉄興業（当時）が安福寺から借地しており、現在は柏原市が安福寺から、年間670万円程の賃貸料で借地している。
==立地環境== [[画像:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|アンティックな音楽堂]] *公園内、東入口付近を中心に「[[玉手山古墳群]]」とよばれる[[古墳]]群が存在する。 *公園内の高台には「アンティックな音楽堂」とよばれる、1908年の開園当時の[[ガゼボ]]がある。明治の洋風建築様式の面影が色濃く残っている。また、天辺に河南鉄道の社印が取り付けられ、現在も残っている。 *かつて玉手山付近と石川の対岸にある[[道明寺 (藤井寺市)|道明寺]]付近にかけては[[大坂の陣#大坂夏の陣|大坂夏の陣]]において[[道明寺の戦い]]が行われた。豊臣方の[[後藤基次|後藤基次（又兵衛）]]は玉手山（旧称：小松山）に陣を敷き、徳川方と戦って戦死した。公園内に両軍戦死者の供養塔、後藤又兵衛の碑や後藤又兵衛しだれ桜がある。 *俳人[[小林一茶]]のゆかりの地でもあり、寛政7年（1795年）4月3日に[[葛井寺]]と道明寺に詣でた後、当時すでに景勝地として有名になっていた玉手山を訪れている。ここで俳句を二句詠んでおり、そのうちの一つを刻んだ句碑が公園内にある。 *敷地内には、[[広域放送|在阪]][[テレビ局]]の[[柏原テレビ中継局]]がある。
==問題点・課題== {{出典の明記|date=2016年10月|section=1}} *掛かりすぎる公園維持管理費 :平成19年度予算で見ると玉手山公園関連維持管理費は3,138万円、これは柏原市の公園維持管理費総額7,627万円の実に41%を占めている。その内柏原市所有の敷地・建物に設置されている有料遊戯具の維持管理も含め業者への管理委託費は2,120万円に上り玉手山公園維持管理費の実に67.4%を占め、柏原市の所有する全公園の維持管理委託費の実に、36.4%（平成19年度）にも上っている。 *市立の一般公園であるにもかかわらず警備上の理由から、夜間は閉門し立ち入り禁止であるため、帰宅後や早朝出勤前のジョギング、ウォーキング等の公園本来の目的に使用できない。 *公共施設であるにもかかわらず、野外劇場などの利用規程がはっきりしていない。 *有料遊戯具の設置に関して委託業者との契約内容が公開されていない。 *来園者の殆どが近隣市町村からの外来者で占められ、柏原市民の利用率が極端に低いこと。 *たのしみ館、昆虫館等の常設館の設置意義や老朽化の激しい野外劇場その他の建造物。
等の問題がある。また、
*観覧車などの大型遊具は撤去されているものの、実態は前身の玉手山遊園地のままであり、ゲーム機、乗り物等の有料遊技具の必要性、大幅な規模縮小も含めた運営形態の見直し *宗教法人との賃貸契約等の見直し
といった課題が挙げられる。
==交通アクセス== *[[近鉄南大阪線]] [[道明寺駅]]下車徒歩約15分 *[[近鉄大阪線]] [[河内国分駅]]下車徒歩約20分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{commons|Category:Tamateyama koen}} *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ 市立玉手山公園] *[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ 玉手山公園と玉手山古墳群、玉手山(安福寺)横穴群]
{{Pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまてやまこうえん}} [[category:大阪府の公園]] [[category:柏原市の地理]] [[Category:1999年開業の施設]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|qq=|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the [[:en:Battle of Dōmyōji|Battle of Domyoji]], Toyotomi retainer [[:en:Gotō Mototsugu|Goto Matabei]] established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|qq=|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Dōmyōji Station|Domyoji Station]] ([[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]) * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] ([[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]])
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|qq=|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the [[:en:Battle of Dōmyōji|Battle of Domyoji]], Toyotomi retainer [[:en:Gotō Mototsugu|Goto Matabei]] established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|qq=|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Dōmyōji Station|Domyoji Station]] ([[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]) * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] ([[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]])
