<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the [[:en:Battle of Dōmyōji|Battle of Domyoji]], Toyotomi retainer [[:en:Gotō Mototsugu|Goto Matabei]] established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Dōmyōji Station|Domyoji Station]] ([[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]) * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] ([[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]])
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the [[:en:Battle of Dōmyōji|Battle of Domyoji]], Toyotomi retainer [[:en:Gotō Mototsugu|Goto Matabei]] established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Dōmyōji Station|Domyoji Station]] ([[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]) * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] ([[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]])
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the [[:en:Battle of Dōmyōji|Battle of Domyoji]], Toyotomi retainer [[:en:Gotō Mototsugu|Goto Matabei]] established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Dōmyōji Station|Domyoji Station]] ([[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]) * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] ([[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]])
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the [[:en:Battle of Dōmyōji|Battle of Domyoji]], Toyotomi retainer [[:en:Gotō Mototsugu|Goto Matabei]] established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet [[:en:Kobayashi Issa|Kobayashi Issa]], who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Dōmyōji Station|Domyoji Station]] ([[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line]]) * 20-minute walk from [[:en:Kawachi-Kokubu Station|Kawachi-Kokubu Station]] ([[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]])

== External Links == {{Commonscat|Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Tamateyama koen}} * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014072300010/ Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] * [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014041800022/ Tamateyama Park and Tamateyama Kofun Group]

Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Dōmyōji Station|en|Dōmyōji Station|WD=Q5321260|lt=Domyoji Station}} ({{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|en|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|WD=Q6379456|lt=Kawachi-Kokubu Station}} ({{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}})

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamateyama koen]][[ja:柏原市立玉手山公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572619]] Wikidata name: Tamateyama Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = {{ill|Urban park|en|Urban park|WD=Q22746|lt=Urban park}} (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the {{ill|Battle of Dōmyōji|en|Battle of Dōmyōji|WD=Q1002716|lt=Battle of Domyoji}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Gotō Mototsugu|en|Gotō Mototsugu|WD=Q2714642|lt=Goto Matabei}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|en|Kobayashi Issa|WD=Q312709|lt=Kobayashi Issa}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Kashiwara TV Relay Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Dōmyōji Station|en|Dōmyōji Station|WD=Q5321260|lt=Domyoji Station}} ({{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|en|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|WD=Q6379456|lt=Kawachi-Kokubu Station}} ({{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}})

The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the {{ill|Battle of Dōmyōji|en|Battle of Dōmyōji|WD=Q1002716|lt=Battle of Domyoji|1=Battle of Dōmyōji}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Gotō Mototsugu|en|Gotō Mototsugu|WD=Q2714642|lt=Goto Matabei|1=Gotō Mototsugu}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|en|Kobayashi Issa|WD=Q312709|lt=Kobayashi Issa|1=Kobayashi Issa}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Q11533663|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Dōmyōji Station|en|Dōmyōji Station|WD=Q5321260|lt=Domyoji Station|1=Dōmyōji Station}} ({{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|en|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|WD=Q6379456|lt=Kawachi-Kokubu Station|1=Kawachi-Kokubu Station}} ({{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}})

{{Unreferenced|date=October 2016}} {{Infobox park |name = Tamateyama Park |japanese_name = 柏原市立玉手山公園 |english_name = Tamateyama Park |image = [[File:玉手山公園中央ゲート01.JPG|300px]] |caption = Central Gate |location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan |latitude = 34|lat_min = 33|lat_sec = 42.7 |longitude = 135|lon_min = 37|lon_sec = 42.0 |type = {{ill|Urban park|en|Urban park|WD=Q22746|lt=Urban park|1=Urban park}} (Comprehensive park) |area = Approximately68,000m<sup>2</sup> |established = [[:en:March 1999|March 1999]] |operator = Kashiwara City Parks and Greenery Division |website = [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/sisetu_t-park.htm Kashiwara City: Tamateyama Park] }}
'''Tamateyama Park''' (柏原市立玉手山公園, Kashiwara Shiritsu Tamateyama Kōen) is a [[Comprehensive park|comprehensive urban park]] located in the Tamateyama hill area of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It opened in March 1999<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Information System]</ref>.
== History and Overview == The park's predecessor was the [[Kintetsu Tamateyama Amusement Park]], established in 1908 by the {{ill|Kawachi Railway|ja|大阪鉄道 (2代目)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Railway|1=Osaka Railway (2nd generation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441654}}, the oldest amusement park in western Japan. The amusement park closed on May 31, 1998, after 90 years of operation. Following a request from the parishioners of {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, the park's landowner, Kashiwara City took over the park’s facilities and reopened it as '''Tamateyama Park''' in March 1999 after a one-year hiatus.
The park spans 68,000 square meters, most of which is still owned by {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|ja|安福寺 (柏原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|qq=}}, a local religious corporation. Kashiwara City leases the land from the temple for approximately 6.7 million yen annually.
== Features == [[File:玉手山公園音楽堂.JPG|thumb|right|Antique Music Hall]] * The park encompasses a cluster of ancient Kofuns known as the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Group|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}. * At the park's elevated areas lies the "Antique Music Hall," a gazebo built in 1908 during the park’s original establishment. It retains the distinct Western-style architectural elements of the Meiji era, with the logo of the Kawachi Railway Company still affixed at its pinnacle. * During the {{ill|Summer Siege of Osaka|ja|大坂夏の陣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Summer Siege of Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大坂の陣|1=Siege of Osaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q475780}} in the {{ill|Battle of Dōmyōji|en|Battle of Dōmyōji|WD=Q1002716|lt=Battle of Domyoji|1=Battle of Dōmyōji}}, Toyotomi retainer {{ill|Gotō Mototsugu|en|Gotō Mototsugu|WD=Q2714642|lt=Goto Matabei|1=Gotō Mototsugu}} established a fortification on Tamateyama (formerly Komatsuyama), where he died in battle. Monuments and memorials for the fallen of both sides, including a weeping cherry tree dedicated to Matabei, are present within the park. * The park is also associated with the poet {{ill|Kobayashi Issa|en|Kobayashi Issa|WD=Q312709|lt=Kobayashi Issa|1=Kobayashi Issa}}, who visited Tamateyama during his travels in 1795. A stone monument inscribed with one of the haiku he composed there stands within the grounds. * The park houses the {{ill|Kashiwara TV Relay Station|ja|柏原テレビ中継局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara TV Relay Station|1=Q11533663|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533663}}, operated by regional television broadcasters.
== Issues and Challenges == {{Unreferenced|date=October 2016|section=1}} * Excessive maintenance costs: In fiscal year 2007, the park's maintenance expenses amounted to 31.38 million yen, comprising 41% of the city's total park maintenance budget. Among this, 21.2 million yen was spent on contracted maintenance services, accounting for 67.4% of the park’s budget. * Restricted nighttime access: The park is locked at night for security reasons, limiting its use for jogging or walking in the early morning or evening. * Lack of clarity in facility usage regulations, such as for the outdoor theater. * Unclear terms in agreements with contractors for managing paid attractions. * Minimal utilization by Kashiwara residents, with most visitors coming from neighboring municipalities. * Deterioration of facilities, such as the outdoor theater and exhibit halls.
Future considerations include evaluating the necessity of attractions, such as arcade games and rides, scaling back operations, and revising lease agreements with the religious corporation.
== Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Dōmyōji Station|en|Dōmyōji Station|WD=Q5321260|lt=Domyoji Station|1=Dōmyōji Station}} ({{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}) * 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|en|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|WD=Q6379456|lt=Kawachi-Kokubu Station|1=Kawachi-Kokubu Station}} ({{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}})
